paragraph: The single-family market in Closter is a real seller's market. With a median sale price of $1,275,000 and nearly half of homes selling above asking, sellers are dictating terms and many deals are drawing multiple offers. The median time on market sits at 17.5 days, and the sale-to-list ratio at 1.0 confirms that homes priced realistically are moving without delay. You'll see the range run from $720,000 to well over $4 million, which tells you Closter holds everything from starter homes to significant estates on deep lots. The condo and townhouse tier is far smaller, with only four sales in the past six months at a median of $1,500,000. This is thin volume, so a single transaction can skew the picture, but the 38-day median days on market suggests these units are moving more deliberately than single-family homes. If you're in the market, understand that single-family inventory moves fast in Closter right now, and pricing competitively matters far more than it does in slower markets. Condos and townhouses here are a niche category, not a mainstream alternative.
